title: Well-formed blameless post-mortem with all required sections
code_snippet:
# Post-mortem: Payment API Outage INC-2024-0142

## Summary
On 2024-03-15, payment-api experienced 15% error rate for 47 min.

## Timeline (UTC, all sourced)
14:18 [TRIGGER] Config deploy (GitHub PR #4521)
14:23 [DETECTION] Error rate spike (Grafana)
14:26 [DETECTION] PagerDuty alert (PD #4821)
14:28 [RESPONSE] On-call acknowledged
14:45 [MITIGATION] Config rolled back (ArgoCD)
15:10 [RECOVERY] All transactions processed

## Root Cause (5-Why, depth 5, systemic)
Empty connection string -> no schema validation -> no pipeline gate review process

## Contributing Factors
Outdated runbook, no auto-rollback, alert went to wrong channel

## Impact: 47 min, ~12K users, 15.2% error rate, ~$48K revenue

## What Went Well
Alert fired in 3 min, correct escalation to DB team

## Action Items (owned, deadlined, tracked)
AI-1 [Prevent] Config validation (JIRA-4521, @platform, Apr 1)
AI-2 [Detect] Redis health check (JIRA-4522, @sre, Mar 22)
AI-3 [Mitigate] Auto-rollback (JIRA-4523, @platform, Apr 15)

## Related: INC-2024-0098, INC-2024-0112
expected_feedback: No violations. Post-mortem demonstrates all quality criteria: (1) blameless language throughout, (2) UTC-sourced timeline with detection/response/recovery phases, (3) 5-Why root cause at depth 5 reaching systemic cause, (4) contributing factors separated from root cause, (5) quantified impact, (6) 'what went well' section, (7) action items with owners, deadlines, categories, and JIRA tickets, (8) related incidents linked.
coverage_rules: Every action item has an owner and deadline
